The Big Picture

See the dashboard →

The news moves fast; progress moves slowly. Zoom out, and the long trends are some of the best good news there is.

Extreme poverty

▼ 85% since 1950
8.5% from 55% in 1950

Share of the world living on less than ~$2.15/day. About half the world was in extreme poverty in 1950; today it's fewer than one in ten.

Source: World Bank, Moatsos / Our World in Data

Child mortality

▼ 83% since 1950
3.8% from 22.5% in 1950

Share of children who die before their fifth birthday. It has fallen in every decade since 1950 — from nearly 1 in 4 to fewer than 1 in 25.

Source: UN IGME / World Bank

Adult literacy

▲ +51 pts since 1950
87% from 36% in 1950

Share of adults who can read and write. It has climbed from roughly a third of the world in 1950 to nearly nine in ten today.

Source: UNESCO / Our World in Data

Reasons to smile

How Cloudbreak works

We comb publishers across the web for the stories that make your day a little brighter, show you the headline and a quick summary, then send you straight to the original source for the full story. We don't republish anyone's work — good news belongs to everyone, and credit belongs to the people who reported it.

Spotted something good?

A neighbor, a rescue, a small win on your street — if it made you smile, send it our way.

Send us a tip →
